title: VPN Split-Tunnel Risk Scoring with Application Correlation
dataSources:
**VPN index (`index=vpn` recommended)** — **`sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`** forwarded by Palo Alto Firewall / Panorama syslog or Cortex Data Lake export ingested via **Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks** (`PAN-OS/GlobalProtect`). Key fields enriched by TA/GP parsing: **`user`/`srcuser`/`Source User`**, **`public_ip` / `Assigned Private IP` / `Public IPv4` aliases**, **`Client OS version`**, explicit **`Split Tunnel` / split-tunnel HIP posture** textual flags (**yes**/no synonyms), **`Stage`/`EventID`** for **`tunnel-connected` equivalents parsed to `action="tunnel-start"` subsets when available—validate field mapping per PAN-OS 10→11 uplift.
 **Cisco ASA / FTD syslog path:** **`sourcetype=cisco:asa`** (**Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA**) capturing **`AnyConnect` session establishment** logs; unify identity into `user` via `FIELDALIAS`; split tunnel may appear indirect—correlate **`Group-Policy`** attributes or ISE downloadable ACL markers when present.
 **Proxy correlator index (`index=proxy`)**: **`sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg`** (**Broadcom / Symantec**, formerly Blue Coat) syslog or WLC exports including **`cs_host`** distinct site cardinality, **`user`** UPN-aligned; alternative Zscaler/normalised web proxy acceptable if field names reconciled (**`hostname`/`user`**).
 **Key fields fused in UC logic:** **`user`**, **`src_ip`/`public_ip`**, **`tunnel_type`** (explicit or inferred), **`split_tunnel` textual yes/no**, **`bytes_sent`**/**`bytes_received`** if augmenting heuristic volume risk later.
 **Volume:** GlobalProtect syslog moderate (**hundreds/sec** multinational); proxy massive (**10k+/sec**)—pre-aggregate the proxy-side `stats` layer using a periodic **summary indexing** saved search or **`tstats` from an accelerated datasummary** populated from `proxy` events. **CIM:** Optional **Authentication** subsets for VPN login success—non-blocking.
app:
**Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks** on HF/indexer parsing tier (**`Splunk_TA_paloalto`** path variant) plus **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA** on same tier.
 **Web proxy Splunk TA** (`TA-bluecoat-proxy`/`TA-symantec-web-security-service` naming varies)—install per vendor guidance.
 **Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud 9.x+.** Global inputs on HF: `UDP:514` reception NOT recommended unsecured—prefer **TLS syslog** forwarding or **splunktcp-ssl** aggregator.
 **Install dirs:** `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_paloalto/`; `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_cisco-asa/`; proxy TA `local/` stanzas mapping `TRANSFORMS` for `user` normalization.
spl:
index=vpn sourcetype IN ("pan:globalprotect","cisco:asa") action="tunnel-start"
| eval source_ip_str=coalesce(public_ip, client_public_ip, src_ip,"unknown")
| join type=left user [
    search index=proxy sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg
    | stats count as direct_web_requests dc(cs_host) as unique_sites by user ]
| fillnull direct_web_requests value=0
| eval risk_score=case(
        split_tunnel="yes" AND direct_web_requests>100,"high",
        split_tunnel="yes" AND direct_web_requests>20,"medium",
        1=1,"low"
      )
| eval risk_priority=case(risk_score="high",3,risk_score="medium",2,1,1)
| where risk_score IN ("high","medium")
| stats values(source_ip_str) as source_ips latest(risk_score) as risk latest(direct_web_requests) as web_requests max(risk_priority) as max_pri by user
| sort - max_pri - web_requests
| fields - max_pri
description: Correlates remote-access tunnel start events that advertise split-tunnel posture with proxied direct-internet browsing volume per user, surfacing identities where sensitive corporate traffic may share a host with under-monitored breakout paths.
value: Security architects can quantify which remote sessions undermine full-tunnel protections, informing policy waivers, agent upgrades, conditional access prompts, or targeted awareness before regulated data egress paths multiply unseen.
implementation: Normalize `user` identity between VPN logs and proxy realms (often UPN versus short name lookups). Maintain `split_tunnel` field extraction parity across Cisco ASA syslog and Palo Alto GlobalProtect versions. Tune direct_web_request ceilings using enterprise browsing baselines sampled over thirty days.
visualization: **Layout:** (1) **Panel A — “Split-tunnel risk table”** — Columns `user`, aggregated `source_ips`, **`risk`**, **`web_requests`**, optional department via lookup. (2) **Panel B — “Risk tier heatmap”** — User cohort (department) vs risk tier counts last 24h. (3) **Panel C — “Tunnel + proxy timeline”** — Overlay VPN `tunnel-start` markers with concurrent proxy volume sparkline (requires stats transform precompute). (4) **Panel D — “Unique destination sites”** — Top `unique_sites` bar for high-risk identities (sanitised hostnames).
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable unless you unify VPN and proxy into a single accelerated network model voluntarily.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Security Monitoring
Palo Alto Networks — GlobalProtect documentation hub
NIST SP 800-46 Rev. 2 — Guide to Enterprise Telework Security
NIST SP 800-114 Rev. 1 — User Remote Access Security

knownFalsePositives: (1) **Developers with legitimate split tunnels** blasting hundreds of NPM/pypi/registry requests while corp apps ride tunnel—policy-approved persona. *Mitigation:* **`engineering_vpn_profiles.csv`** lowers risk tier and raises request ceilings for correlated AD group `engineering`. (2) **Video conferencing preferring UDP direct egress** increments proxy counters unevenly—not true data exfil. *Mitigation:* ignore dominant conferencing CDNs maintained in a recurring ASN allowlist lookup. (3) **Shared kiosk accounts** multiplex many humans—the split tunnel heuristic is unreliable without asset context. *Mitigation:* optionally enrich with workstation serial fields from MDM. (4) **Proxy log sampling / missing shards** artificially lowers counted direct web bursts. *Mitigation:* alert on indexer lag and uneven proxy ingest volume **before** trusting low counts. (5) **Stale HIP posture** after patching can keep `split_tunnel="yes"` longer than firewall reality. *Mitigation:* correlate GlobalProtect HIP last-report freshness fields when logged. (6) **DNS-over-HTTPS and encrypted proxy visibility gaps** distort `unique_sites`; do not automate punitive actions solely on diminished hostname diversity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **Splunk Palo Alto Networks Add-on** and **Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA** indexing **identity-rich GlobalProtect / AnyConnect** transcripts into **`index=vpn`** with consistent `_time` zone **UTC**.
• **ProxySG / web gateway** telemetry in **`index=proxy`** with **`user`** (UPN-preferred), **`cs_host`**, **`sc-status`** / result codes validated weekly against vendor UI.
• **Identity bridging lookup** **`idm_vpn_to_proxy.csv`** mapping `SHORTNAME@vpn_field` ⇄ `firstname.lastname@corp.example` proxy identities—refresh nightly from an authoritative IdP export on a guarded share.
• **Firewall policy workbook** translating **HIP profile `split_tunnel` yes/no** to enterprise risk tier expectations—signed quarterly by Networking.
• **Performance budget:** brute `join` search expensive—design **proxy summary macro** prerequisite already partially described in **dataSources** volume note.
• **Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud 9.2+.**

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**GlobalProtect syslog:** PAN device config **Device → Server Profiles → Syslog → Splunk_SSL** transporting **HIP + GlobalProtect** logs; Splunk TA handles line breaking `LINE_BREAKER` for PAN 11 multi-line escapes.
**Cisco ASA:** enable VPN-related syslog IDs appropriate to your ASA software release toward the heavy forwarder; correlate `%ASA-*` tunnel-up signals with Splunk `_raw` samples documented internally.
**Proxy:** enable **Squid-class style** Extended Log Fields including authenticated user—even if anonymity policies exist elsewhere, corporate-managed devices should populate user for policy enforcement.
**Identity transforms:** Implement `EVAL-proxy_user_normalized = lower(proxy_user)` and `EVAL-vpn_user_normalized = mvindex(split(user,"\\"),-1)` for legacy DOMAIN\USER bridging join key **after** KV lookup bridging if necessary.
**Verification SPL:** Parallel `gentimes` anchored last hour count join results vs exploratory `| multisearch [| search index=vpn earliest=-60m sourcetype IN ("pan:globalprotect","cisco:asa") | stats dc(user) as vpn_users] [| search index=proxy earliest=-60m sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg | stats dc(user) as px_users]`

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype IN ("pan:globalprotect","cisco:asa") action="tunnel-start"
| eval source_ip_str=coalesce(public_ip, client_public_ip, src_ip,"unknown")
| join type=left user [
    search index=proxy sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg
    | stats count as direct_web_requests dc(cs_host) as unique_sites by user ]
| fillnull direct_web_requests value=0
| eval risk_score=case(
        split_tunnel="yes" AND direct_web_requests>100,"high",
        split_tunnel="yes" AND direct_web_requests>20,"medium",
        1=1,"low"
      )
| eval risk_priority=case(risk_score="high",3,risk_score="medium",2,1,1)
| where risk_score IN ("high","medium")
| stats values(source_ip_str) as source_ips latest(risk_score) as risk latest(direct_web_requests) as web_requests max(risk_priority) as max_pri by user
| sort - max_pri - web_requests
| fields - max_pri
```

Understanding this SPL
• **Tunnel-start filter** restricts to beginnings—prevents teardown duplicates reinflating join sets; teardown variant optional extension not shown.
• **`source_ip_str` coalesce** tolerates heterogeneous vendor field spelling differences.
• **Subsearch `stats`** pre-aggregates proxy noise—production should replace literal subsearch with **`| lookup proxy_user_hourly_aggregate ...`** summary for scale.
• **`fillnull`** ensures untracked proxies not mistaken as zero-trust absolution—interpret carefully: absent row means **lack of visibility**, not innocence—document in SOP.
• **`risk_score` case tiers** heuristic default—tune thresholds after **30d baseline** percentile study (`perc75(direct_web_requests)`).
• **`risk_priority` numeric encode** simplifies `sort`; dropped after sorting via `fields -` housekeeping.

Schedule as Alert: cron **`37 */8 * * *`**; time range **`earliest=-4h@m`**, **`latest=@m`**; throttle duplicate `user` for **8 hours**; optional SOAR ticket **gather-only**—no automated firewall changes without human approval.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Choose user risk high; correlate Panorama Monitor → GlobalProtect **`Client`** session details.
(b) Pivot proxy reporting **Username** trending same hour verifying counts ±10%.
(c) Inspect **HIP profile** verifying split tunnel factual vs cached UI lie.
(d) Synthetic test: authorised lab account purposely split + scripted curl loops validate detection fires.
(e) Negative control: forced full tunnel user should degrade to medium/low unaffected path.
(f) ASA-only environment lacking `split_tunnel` field manual mapping from **group-policy** label—validate static translation table correctness.

Step 4 — Operationalize
**Dashboard:** Quartet per `visualization`. Add textual **risk narrative** Markdown panel outlining policy citations **NIST 800-46** segmentation expectations.
**Alerting:** Email **ZTNA committee** Slack bridge; escalate high risk only if tandem DLP correlates—not shown but recommended cross-index join macro `macro_dlp_user_hits_last_24h`.
**Runbook:** (1) Confirm authorised engineering waiver file. (2) Offer user remedial patching / profile refresh. (3) If unauthorised, escalate **Manager + HR sensitivity** calibrated regionally. (4) Firewall policy tighten trial on lab group—not mass change. (5) Post-incident metrics feed programme reporting split adoption KPIs.

• **Stale TA field extractions after PAN-OS upgrades** — reinstall or patch the Palo Alto TA and revisit `props.conf` overrides from release notes.
• **Join cardinality explosion / timeouts** — implement summary indexing for proxy aggregates (mandatory at scale).
• **Case-sensitive `user` mismatch** — normalize with `lower()` on both VPN and proxy sides before joining.
• **Proxy anonymity / missing `user`** — treat unusually high anonymous-ratio hours as data-quality incidents, not as proof of innocence for individuals.
• **Session refresh lag** — users may retain risk scores until tunnels restart; communicate expected TTL behaviour in awareness material.
grandmaExplanation: When people work remotely, sometimes part of their internet skips the guarded tunnel while the rest rides inside it. We connect those dots with everyday browsing traces so guardians notice risky mixes before leaks outrun policy.
